candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When internal regime is MOMENTUM but TradingView reports zero confirming signals in the last 2h, the 75% sizing multiplier is too generous — internal-only MOMENTUM with no external confirmation should size at 50% or freeze until a TV signal appears.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: MOMENTUM regimes are durable only when multiple independent data sources agree on direction. A single-source MOMENTUM read with no external corroboration is statistically indistinguishable from a chop regime that the internal model has mislabeled — institutional flow that creates genuine momentum leaves footprints across multiple venues, so the absence of any TV signal means the move is likely retail/noise-driven and mean-reverting.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if internal_regime == 'MOMENTUM' and tv_signal_count_2h == 0: sizing_multiplier = min(sizing_multiplier, 0.50)
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Reduces variance of returns during false-MOMENTUM periods; estimated Sharpe +0.02 to +0.04 via lower denominator volatility on unconfirmed signals.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: Current state shows Internal=MOMENTUM, TradingView=UNKNOWN (0 signals), sizing at 75% — yet 30-indicator consensus reads NEUTRAL/FLAT across all 5 pairs (conv 8-25%, hist_hr=0%, green_matches=0), directly contradicting the MOMENTUM label. This is a live false-positive MOMENTUM read being sized at 75%.

reflection.system_health_note: Zero trades in last 7 days with Regime=UNKNOWN and a contradicted MOMENTUM label (consensus NEUTRAL/FLAT, hist_hr=0%) — system is correctly in a low-conviction holding pattern, but the 75% sizing on unconfirmed MOMENTUM is the main exposure risk worth gating before the next signal fires.
